runs.question: Can inflation be modeled as a predictive timer for socio-political and economic events?
supported: Food inflation >15-20% preceded protests in selective cases (Arab Spring, Sri Lanka)
General inflation >30-40% preceded instability in some historical cases
Counter-examples exist: Turkey 80%+, Argentina chronic, Venezuela survived
rejected: Inflation alone is sufficient for prediction
Universal thresholds apply across all countries
Higher inflation always means more unrest
unknown: Observer effect: does prediction change outcomes
Whether hypotheses generalize beyond selective cases

novel_hypotheses.name: Cascade Resonance
novel_hypotheses.key_metric: Frequency effects in simulation (not empirically validated)

runs.peer_review_fix: 2026-03-16: Relabeled CONFIRMED → HYPOTHESIS after critique about overclaiming from toy simulations

key_insights: Context determines outcomes - legitimacy reservoir is key (post-hoc explanation, unfalsifiable)
Inflation is necessary but not sufficient
Composites may outperform single indicators (simulation only)
Populations may adapt to chronic inflation (untested)
Frequency/timing of inflation may matter as much as level (simulation only)
limitations: No empirical validation - simulations prove math consistency only
Post-hoc flexibility - explanations invented after seeing data
Selection bias - cases chosen because they fit pattern
Endogeneity unsolved - cannot isolate causal direction
